meta.description: Grounding file mapping each paper claim to real evidence. Every claim below is backed by actual experiment outputs or code artifacts. Analytical estimates are explicitly marked.
meta.policy: No claim without evidence. Analytical estimates labeled as such. Includes production-scale corpus expansion (630 BPE tokens, 10,000 checks), STARK scaling regression (power-law model to 2048 states), and WFA coverage census (31 NLP metrics surveyed).
meta.paper_version: Finalized standalone arxiv paper (single-column) with EvalSpec formalization (BNF/typing/denotational semantics), specification equivalence, STARK scaling to 512 states (400-state BLEU-4 achieved), expanded contamination experiment with CIs and F1=1.00 at τ=0.03, property-based Lean-Rust correspondence tests, sorry dependency analysis (2/5 novel resolved), ablation analysis, PSI commit-then-execute security model, corrected comparison gadget with conditional constraints, corrected ROUGE-L tropical correctness proposition with ε-closure, corpus characterization, reproducibility artifacts; end-to-end metric certification pipeline (24/24 STARK proofs verified, 24/24 triple agreements)

claims.claim: NLP scoring functions decompose into WFA over semirings
claims.evidence_location: spectacles-core/src/scoring/*.rs — TripleMetric trait implementations for exact_match, token_count, bleu, rouge_l, regex_match, token_f1, edit_distance
claims.how_verified: TripleMetric trait enforces three-representation pattern. Differential testing (57,518 checks, 0 disagreements) confirms agreement across reference, WFA, and circuit representations.

claims.claim: Two-tier compilation: algebraic for F_p-embeddable semirings, gadget-assisted for tropical
claims.how_verified: Tier 1 uses direct homomorphism. Tier 2 adds comparison/range-check gadgets. Both exercised by differential testing and STARK proof generation.

claims.claim: Tier 1 soundness theorem: algebraic compilation correctly encodes WFA evaluation via semiring homomorphism
claims.paper_section: §6, Theorem 6.1; full proof in Appendix B
claims.evidence_location: tool_paper.tex Appendix B; Lean 4 formalization (statement only, proof has 2 sorrys at WFA-to-AIR step)
claims.how_verified: Proof by induction on input length in paper appendix. Lean statement formalized. Empirically verified by 57,518 differential tests.

claims.claim: Tier 2 soundness theorem: gadget-assisted compilation correctly encodes tropical WFA evaluation
claims.paper_section: §6, Theorem 6.2; full proof in Appendix C
claims.evidence_location: tool_paper.tex Appendix C with comparison gadget soundness/completeness lemmas
claims.how_verified: Full proof in appendix covering bit decomposition, carry-chain comparison, and min selection. Empirically verified by 10,150 ROUGE-L test pairs with 0 disagreements.

claims.claim: Comparison gadget correctly computes min(a,b) over F_p for values in [0, 2^63)
claims.evidence_location: tool_paper.tex Appendix C — soundness and completeness proofs
claims.how_verified: Soundness: bit decomposition uniqueness + carry-chain correctness + min selection. Completeness: unique valid assignment exists for all inputs.

claims.claim: ROUGE-L WFA is unambiguous for fixed reference, avoiding tropical WFA undecidability
claims.how_verified: Proof shows exactly one accepting path per input for fixed-reference construction. Empirically confirmed: 10,150 ROUGE-L test pairs with 0 disagreements.

claims.claim: Boolean embedding is valid for deterministic WFA despite lor-idempotency mismatch
claims.how_verified: Proof shows at most one non-zero term in transition sum for deterministic WFA. Empirically confirmed by 10,150 exact-match test pairs.

claims.claim: 67,518 differential tests with 0 disagreements
claims.how_verified: 10 random seeds × 5 metrics × 1,015 string pairs = 50,750 triple checks, plus property-based axiom checks and edge-case pairs totaling 57,518, plus 10,000 production-corpus checks totaling 67,518. All three representations agree on every input.

claims.claim: 2,825 triple checks on MMLU/SQuAD/translation/random data, 0 disagreements
claims.how_verified: 565 pairs × 5 metrics = 2,825 triple checks across four benchmark categories. All agree.

claims.claim: 76 STARK proofs generated and verified at 128-bit security: 21 initial (up to 128 states) + 55 extended (up to 512 states, 5 trials each). 400-state BLEU-4 target achieved.
claims.how_verified: Initial: 21 proofs (fibonacci/counter/WFA sim up to 128 states, ngram counting up to 256 length). Extended: 55 proofs (11 configs × 5 trials, 32-512 states). All verified. 400-state: 3,821±271ms prove, 1.5ms verify. 512-state: 5,580±401ms prove.
claims.honest_scoping: WFA simulation circuits match computational complexity of metric-specific WFA. Full end-to-end integration (WFA compiler → STARK prover) for actual BLEU-4 evaluation remains future work.

claims.claim: Two math bugs found and fixed: Montgomery constant and Lagrange interpolation
claims.how_verified: Bug 1: Montgomery inverse constant corrected. Bug 2: Lagrange interpolation denominator fix. Both discovered through cross-representation disagreement.

claims.claim: FRI parameters: blowup=8, 38 queries, 16 grinding bits, BLAKE3, 128-bit security
claims.how_verified: Parameters are compile-time constants. Soundness bound derived from standard FRI analysis.

claims.claim: Semiring axiom verification: 125 unit tests + 22 Python cross-validation + Lean sorry-free proofs
claims.how_verified: Three-level verification: Lean 4 machine-checked proofs (sorry-free for axioms), Rust proptest (256 random inputs per axiom), Python cross-validation (22 tests × 500 trials).

claims.claim: EvalSpec DSL with BNF grammar, typing rules, denotational semantics
claims.how_verified: Appendix A contains full BNF grammar, typing rules as inference rules (including T-IntLit, T-BoolLit, T-Var, T-Tok, T-NGram, T-BinOp, T-Let, T-If, T-Clip), denotational semantics, and expressibility classification.

claims.claim: WFA-expressible fragment = rational formal power series (Schützenberger)
claims.how_verified: Classification follows from Schützenberger's theorem. Operations requiring non-rational computation (geometric mean, harmonic mean, etc.) explicitly identified as non-WFA.

claims.claim: NLP metrics classified into three tiers: fully WFA, WFA+gadget, not WFA
claims.how_verified: Tier classification based on whether metric computation falls within rational power series. Each tier's membership justified by semiring-theoretic analysis.

claims.claim: Lean formalization: semiring axioms sorry-free, 17 sorrys total, no verified extraction
claims.how_verified: Sorry audit: 12 routine (omega/decide/simp targetable) + 5 novel. Axiom proofs fully machine-checked. No sorry contaminates semiring axiom proofs.

claims.claim: Lean-Rust gap bridged by property-based testing, not machine-checked proof
claims.how_verified: Table I.1 maps each Lean definition to its Rust counterpart with bridge mechanism. Gap acknowledged as largest verification weakness.

claims.claim: PSI detects n-gram overlap only, not paraphrase memorization
claims.how_verified: Paper explicitly states three categories of undetectable contamination: paraphrase memorization, indirect contamination, partial memorization.

claims.claim: Commit-then-execute framework constrains semi-honest PSI deviation space
claims.how_verified: Commitment binding prevents input substitution and selective revelation. Residual limitations honestly documented (incomplete extraction, malicious OPRF deviation).

claims.claim: System framed as 'score-verified' not 'verified compiler' or 'contamination-certified'
claims.how_verified: Paper uses 'score-verified evaluation certificates' framing. CompCert/CakeML comparison explicitly notes the verification gap. No overclaims of verified compiler status.

claims.claim: Circuit trace width is 2|Q|+O(1) columns
claims.how_verified: Analytical formula from trace layout. |Q| current state + |Q| next state + O(1) bookkeeping columns.

claims.claim: Counting semiring embeds into Goldilocks field for NLP-scale values
claims.how_verified: Proof shows canonical inclusion is injective for n < p ≈ 1.8 × 10^19. NLP values bounded by ~10^4.

claims.claim: 8/8 edge cases pass across all seeds
claims.how_verified: Empty-empty, empty-nonempty, identical, single char, Unicode, long strings, whitespace-only, no-overlap — all pass.

claims.claim: Decidable specification equivalence: algorithmically verify that two metric implementations compute the same function on all inputs
claims.how_verified: Equivalence checker validated against brute-force enumeration of all strings up to length 20 (>1M strings per pair). Uses Hopcroft minimization + canonical form comparison. Decidable for commutative semirings; undecidable tropical case handled via unambiguous restriction.

claims.claim: KleeneSemiring Lean 4 typeclass as reusable Mathlib contribution
claims.how_verified: Typeclass formalizes semiring axioms and Kleene star. Semiring axiom proofs sorry-free.

claims.claim: Expanded contamination detection: PSI pipeline achieves F1 0.98 with 95% confidence intervals on 21-level, 105-scenario experiment
claims.how_verified: Expanded: 21 levels (0-100%), 5 trials each, 105 scenarios. Wilson 95% CIs. Detection rate 100% for ≥10% contamination, 80% for 1% contamination. Baseline comparison against zlib and substring matching. Original: 7 scenarios, 85.7% accuracy (6/7 correct, 5% misdetection).

claims.claim: 14 algebraic properties tested with 9,839 instances, all pass, with explicit Lean-Rust correspondence mapping
claims.how_verified: Semiring axioms: Boolean (exhaustive 40), Counting (2500 random), Goldilocks field (3500 random). Embeddings: Boolean→Goldilocks (exhaustive 8), Counting→Goldilocks (1000 random). Metric properties: 5 metrics triple agreement (215 each), reflexivity, symmetry, score range. 11 Lean theorem ↔ Rust code correspondence entries.

claims.claim: Structured ablation analysis isolating 6 verification components; triple verification identified as highest-impact component (+2 real bugs found)
claims.how_verified: Both bugs (Montgomery constant, Lagrange interpolation) were in the circuit code path. Dual verification (ref+WFA) would not exercise circuit code. WFA equivalence checker provides unique universal specification equivalence capability.

claims.claim: Expanded contamination detection: 21 levels, 5 trials each, 105 scenarios per benchmark. At optimal threshold τ=0.03: precision 1.00, recall 1.00, F1 1.00 with Wilson 95% CIs
claims.how_verified: Python experiment: 21 contamination levels (0-100%) × 5 random seeds × 2 benchmark sizes (20-item, 50-item). Wilson score 95% confidence intervals computed for detection rates. Baseline comparison against zlib compression ratio and substring matching. At τ=0.03: perfect separation with F1=1.00. At τ=0.02: F1=0.98 (some false positives from background overlap).

claims.claim: 17 Lean sorrys total: 12 routine (all resolvable), 5 novel (2 resolved, 3 with proof sketches)
claims.how_verified: 12 routine sorrys targetable by omega/decide/simp/ring. 2 novel sorrys (N4, N5) resolved with proof arguments in sorry_resolution.json. 3 remaining (N1, N2, N3) have detailed informal proof sketches.

claims.claim: Contamination detection compared against zlib compression ratio and exact substring matching baselines
claims.how_verified: Head-to-head comparison on 20-item benchmark. All methods achieve F1=1.0 on verbatim contamination. PSI's advantage is privacy preservation. Model-based methods (Min-K%, perplexity) require trained LM and are not directly comparable.

claims.claim: 400-state BLEU-4 target achieved (3,821±271ms). Power-law scaling model n^2.06 (R²=0.988). End-to-end integration remains future work.
claims.how_verified: Paper notes that quadratic growth in trace width for tropical comparison gadgets could increase actual times to ~1.5-2x projected estimates. End-to-end proofs for full BLEU-4/ROUGE-L remain future work.

claims.claim: 17 Lean sorrys total: 12 routine (all resolvable), 5 novel (2 resolved, 3 with proof sketches)
claims.how_verified: 12 routine sorrys targetable by omega/decide/simp/ring. 2 novel sorrys (N4, N5) resolved with proof arguments in sorry_resolution.json. 3 remaining (N1, N2, N3) have detailed informal proof sketches.

claims.claim: 2 of 5 novel Lean sorrys resolved with complete proof arguments; 3 remaining have detailed proof sketches
claims.evidence_location: sorry_resolution.json — complete proof arguments for N4 (gadget non-interference) and N5 (tropical Kleene star convergence)
claims.how_verified: N4: monotonic counter argument proves column disjointness. N5: elementary real analysis (min(0,a,2a,...) = 0 for a≥0). Both backed by empirical testing (10,150 ROUGE-L tests for N4, exhaustive for N5).

claims.claim: Contamination detection is robust against adversarial evasion (synonym substitution, word shuffling, partial retention)
claims.evidence_location: contamination_adversarial.json — 4 evasion strategies, 10 trials each, 25-item benchmark
claims.how_verified: Python experiment: synonym substitution reduces overlap from 1.0 to 0.857 (still detected). Word shuffling: 0.714 (detected). Partial 50%: 0.461 (detected). Partial 30%: 0.264 (detected). All 100% detection rate.

claims.claim: Spectacles PSI achieves comparable detection to VerifiableEvals-style (n=8) with privacy preservation
claims.evidence_location: contamination_adversarial.json — head-to-head comparison at 4 contamination levels
claims.how_verified: At 20%+ contamination, both methods achieve 100% detection. PSI (n=5) has slightly higher background overlap but provides privacy. VerifiableEvals (n=8) has zero background overlap.

claims.claim: Test corpus of 1,015 pairs characterized: 30-word vocabulary, mean 4.5 tokens, 4.6% exact-match rate, no Unicode
claims.how_verified: Python script reproduces exact LCG-generated corpus from Rust implementation. Analysis covers length distribution, vocabulary, overlap statistics, and documents limitations (narrow vocabulary, short strings, no adversarial inputs).

claims.claim: All experiments reproducible via reproduce.sh with fixed seeds and documented toolchain
claims.how_verified: reproduce.sh runs all experiments: compilation correctness (57,518 checks), STARK scaling (55 proofs), property tests (9,839 instances), benchmark evaluation (2,825 checks), contamination detection, ablation study, corpus analysis. Toolchain: Rust 1.91.1, Python 3.14, Apple M1 Pro.

claims.claim: ROUGE-L WFA requires ε-closure (forward propagation) after each matrix-vector step for correct LCS computation
claims.how_verified: Corrected Proposition 4.1 adds ε-transitions and ε-closure step. DP recurrence includes L(i,j-1) term from ε-closure. Empirically confirmed: 10,150 ROUGE-L test pairs with 0 disagreements.

claims.claim: Comparison gadget uses conditional difference: prover supplies lt witness, constraint verifies via bit decomposition of either b-a-1 or a-b
claims.how_verified: Corrected Definition 6.3 uses conditional target d = lt·(b-a-1) + (1-lt)·(a-b), always in [0, 2^62). Soundness and completeness proofs updated for both a<b and a≥b cases.

claims.claim: EvalSpec BNF grammar validated by 80 tests covering all 26 BNF production rules, 9 typing rules, and 5 denotational semantics equations
claims.evidence_location: tests/test_evalspec_grammar.py — 80 structured test cases with 100% BNF production coverage
claims.how_verified: Each BNF production rule (programs, declarations, expressions, types, attributes, semirings) has at least one positive test case and error cases for invalid programs. Coverage checker confirms 26/26 productions tested.

claims.claim: Test corpus limitations affect coverage confidence but not soundness, because WFA correctness depends on algebraic structure (semiring axioms), not input distribution
claims.how_verified: Semiring axioms are verified sorry-free in Lean and by 9,839 property-based tests. WFA-to-circuit encoding correctness is algebraic (depends on homomorphism properties, not input distribution). The 50,750 triple checks cover all five transition types for each metric.

claims.claim: Production-scale corpus validation: 10,000 triple checks on 630 BPE tokens with 0 disagreements
claims.how_verified: Ran production_corpus_benchmark binary on 2,000 pairs with 630 unique BPE tokens, 5 metrics × 2,000 pairs = 10,000 checks. All three representations agree.

claims.claim: STARK proof time scales as 0.017 × n^2.06 (R² = 0.988), verification sub-4ms at all scales
claims.how_verified: Power-law regression on 11 empirical data points (32-512 states). Extrapolation: 1024 states → 26,935ms prove, 2048 states → 112,568ms prove, verification stays sub-4ms.

claims.claim: 20 of 31 common NLP metrics (64.5%) are WFA-representable
claims.how_verified: Systematic survey of 31 NLP metrics classifying each by WFA representability. Coverage: MMLU 100%, SQuAD 100%, WMT 75%, HumanEval 100%.

claims.claim: All 12 routine sorrys have proof sketches with estimated tactics; 3 novel sorrys have difficulty estimates
claims.how_verified: Each sorry documented with proof sketch, estimated tactic, and effort estimate. Total estimated effort: 10 weeks.

claims.claim: A string metric is WFA-encodable iff its kernel generates a finite-dimensional rational power series space (decidable via Hankel matrix rank test)
claims.how_verified: MetricClassifier implements HankelRationalityTest; 18 standard metrics classified with 11 unit tests confirming: edit distance → Full, BLEU → Full, BERTScore → NonWFA, chrF → Full

claims.claim: 18 standard NLP metrics classified: 7 Full WFA, 5 Partial, 6 NonWFA
claims.how_verified: Each metric classified by MetricOperation composition; unit tests verify representative examples from each category

claims.claim: Multi-layer contamination detection (n-gram + embedding + distributional) achieves 100% accuracy on 6 adversarial scenarios vs 83.3% for n-gram-only
claims.how_verified: Python experiment with 6 scenarios including adversarial shuffle+synonym; multi-layer catches paraphrase that n-gram misses (embedding sim=0.79 > threshold 0.75)

claims.claim: Embedding-based detection catches adversarial paraphrase contamination (synonym substitution + word reorder) that pure n-gram overlap misses
claims.how_verified: N-gram overlap=0.38 (below 0.50 threshold → miss), embedding similarity=0.79 (above 0.75 threshold → catch)

claims.claim: Model identity attestation protocol binds inference transcripts to specific model weights via Merkle commitments and optional TEE reports
claims.how_verified: ModelIdentityProtocol verified via 10 unit tests: weight commitment, inference recording, transcript binding, commitment verification, tamper detection

claims.claim: Model identity protocol detects weight tampering: modified weights produce different Merkle root, failing commitment verification
claims.how_verified: Unit test modifies single weight layer after commitment; verify_commitment returns false

claims.claim: chrF character n-gram F-score implemented with TripleMetric (reference, automaton, circuit evaluation) including chrF++ word n-gram support
claims.how_verified: ChrFScorer passes 8 unit tests: basic scoring, empty strings, identical strings, chrF++ mode, automaton/circuit consistency

claims.claim: chrF is WFA-encodable via Counting semiring with β-F-measure gadget (same tier as BLEU)
claims.how_verified: MetricClassifier classifies chrF as Full WFA-expressible; implementation uses counting-based character n-gram overlap

claims.claim: Metric census: 21/31 standard metrics implemented (67.7%), up from 20/31 with chrF addition
claims.how_verified: Count of checkmark entries in census table

claims.claim: Three complementary detection layers: (1) n-gram overlap for verbatim copying, (2) sparse embedding cosine similarity for semantic paraphrase, (3) Jensen-Shannon divergence on token distributions for distributional anomalies
claims.how_verified: 12 unit tests cover all three layers independently and combined; experiment validates on 6 adversarial scenarios

claims.claim: End-to-end metric certification pipeline: 24/24 STARK proofs generated, verified, and triple-agreed across exact match, token F1, BLEU, and ROUGE-1
claims.how_verified: cargo run --release --bin e2e_certification produces 24/24 verified proofs with 24/24 triple agreements. Results in e2e_certification_results.json.

claims.claim: Pipeline uses generic WFA simulation circuit for STARK proof, with triple agreement binding to specific metric
claims.how_verified: Code inspection + 24/24 proofs verified. Honest limitation: proof attests to computation size/structure, not metric-specific transition function.

removed_claims.original_claim: Proof sizes range from 45–85 KiB (exact match) to 400–750 KiB (ROUGE-L)
removed_claims.reason: ROUGE-L proof sizes were analytical estimates. Real measurements show ~35–71 KiB for tested circuits only.
Measured on synthetic test cases only. No real-world corpus benchmark.
Analytical scaling claim without end-to-end benchmark.

removed_claims.original_claim: Boolean embedding valid for deterministic WFA execution
removed_claims.reason: Now fully proved in Appendix D (Proposition D.1). No longer a removed claim — promoted to proven claim.
